steps.title: Identify the connecting principle
steps.content: The first study shows that genetic differences explain behavioral differences between breeds—especially for attachment and attention-seeking.

steps.title: Apply to the breed-specific finding
steps.content:
The second study found that French bulldogs and cairn terriers differ in attachment and attention-seeking.

Since genetic variation explains this behavioral difference between breeds, these two breeds must differ in the genetic basis for this trait.

Choice A directly captures this implication. The other choices discuss temporal genetic change (B), absolute comparative levels (C), or individual variation (D), none of which follow from the evidence.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
